meta.note: FICTIONAL demonstration data. Not a real person, company or deal. ins-s08 exists to demonstrate the confidentiality filter catching employer material.

items.title: Three CRMs in five years means the CRM was never the problem
items.raw: Founder told me she has migrated CRM three times in five years and trusts none of them. Every migration was sold internally as the fix. The actual problem was that sales, marketing and delivery never agreed what a qualified deal was. The tool keeps taking the blame for a definitions problem.

reasons: No confidentiality signals detected. Still confirm nothing identifies a client, colleague or live deal.
confidentiality.note: This is a suggested classification for Stuart to confirm, not a verdict.

items.fictionNote: FICTIONAL demonstration data. Not a real person, company or deal.

items.title: Discount authority is a leadership instrument, not a finance rule
items.raw: Watched a deal review where a rep discounted 22% to close by quarter end. Nobody could say who approved it. Where discount tiers exist and are enforced, margin holds and negotiations get shorter, because the seller stops negotiating against themselves.

reasons: No confidentiality signals detected. Still confirm nothing identifies a client, colleague or live deal.
confidentiality.note: This is a suggested classification for Stuart to confirm, not a verdict.

items.title: Sponsors renew on outcomes they can show their boss
items.raw: Sponsorship renewal conversations keep failing when the package was logo placement. The renewals that close themselves are the ones where the sponsor walked away with a number they could put in their own board deck.

reasons: No confidentiality signals detected. Still confirm nothing identifies a client, colleague or live deal.

items.title: AI pilots die where the workflow was never mapped
items.raw: Every stalled AI adoption story I hear in Singapore SME circles has the same shape: tool bought, prompt shared in Slack, nobody changed the workflow. The teams getting value mapped the workflow first and slotted AI into named steps with an owner.

reasons: No confidentiality signals detected. Still confirm nothing identifies a client, colleague or live deal.

items.title: The founder bottleneck shows up in the CRM before it shows up in revenue
items.raw: In founder-led businesses the tell is deals sitting in "waiting on founder" stages. Operating drag is measurable months before the revenue line feels it.

items.title: Pipeline reviews that read the dashboard aloud are theatre
items.raw: Sat through a review where the leader read the dashboard to the room. No decision was taken. A pipeline review should end with stage moves, kills and next actions or it is a weather report.

items.title: Event P&Ls hide the media asset inside them
items.raw: Most event businesses sit on an audience asset they only monetise two days a year. The media layer (newsletter, podcast, research) is the recurring revenue nobody staffs.

items.title: Category creation is mostly patience plus vocabulary

items.title: The diagnostic is the product
items.raw: Advisory conversations keep proving it: the paid diagnostic that names the problem precisely is worth more to the buyer than the fix, because it converts anxiety into a plan.

items.title: Fractional leadership fails without a decision framework
items.raw: Fractional roles collapse when every decision still routes through the founder. The fix is a written decision framework in week one: what the fractional leader decides alone, consults on, or escalates.

items.raw: A publisher raising prices found the objection was never budget, it was comparison. Reframed the rate card around outcomes and categories they owned; the same numbers stopped being questioned.

items.title: iGaming supplier pricing is where discipline goes to die
items.raw: Supplier deals in iGaming still run on relationship pricing and side letters. The first supplier to run clean tiered packaging will look expensive and win anyway, because procurement can finally compare.

items.title: Question: what does a "qualified conversation" actually mean
items.raw: If the whole engine optimises for qualified conversations, the definition needs to be written down: right person, real problem, honest interest in discussing it. Otherwise the metric inflates like impressions did.

items.title: NEXTPredict Master Social Schedule, Updated
items.raw:
# NEXTPredict Master Social Schedule, Updated

The workbook contains 103 calendar items, including 49 proprietary data-journalism projects and nine additions from the final 13-14 July research sweep.

## Added from the final 13-14 July research sweep

| Date | Channel | Topic | Status | Verification note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 15 Jul 2026 | X | Prediction markets marketed like sportsbooks | Ready | Based on two New York Post promotions using the same NYPMAX code |
| 17 Jul 2026 | Stuart LinkedIn | Love Island and the spoiler supply chain | Ready | Separate reported concerns, public rumours and confirmed statements |
| 20 Jul 2026 | Prediction Markets Forum | Prediction prices inside ordinary sports journalism | Ready | Timestamp the market and treat transfer reports as rumours |
| 21 Jul 2026 | X | Goldman Sachs draws a contract-specific compliance line | Ready | Policy predates the sweep; do not frame recirculation as a new 13 July announcement |
| 23 Jul 2026 | Prediction Markets Forum | When the market thinks, how many traders is that? | Ready | Read the underlying UC Berkeley paper before publication |
| 24 Jul 2026 | X | Blockchain.com embeds Polymarket | Unverified watch | No official source found |
| 28 Jul 2026 | Stuart LinkedIn | The Probability Citation Tracker | Planned | Publish the scoring rubric before naming or ranking outlets |
| 29 Jul 2026 | X | Cross-venue arbitrage dashboards | Unverified watch | Prove both legs were executable after fees and rule alignment |
| 31 Jul 2026 | X | Growth forecasts and Prospect / Foris DAX FCM lead | Unverified watch | Require an official filing, signed LOI or company release |

### Verification rule

The three unverified items are intentionally present in the operating schedule as monitoring tasks. They are not approved claims and should not be posted until a primary source, filing or direct product evidence is captured.

anonymisationChecklist: Company names replaced with a role description (e.g. "a B2B events business").
Individual names removed or replaced with roles.
Exact commercial figures bucketed ("six figures", "double-digit uplift") or removed.
Private internal decisions and disagreements removed.
